chunks.title: WHO Physical Activity Recommendations
chunks.content:
WHO Global Guidelines on Physical Activity (2020):
Adults 18-64 years:
- At least 150-300 min/week of moderate-intensity aerobic activity (brisk walking, cycling, swimming), OR
- At least 75-150 min/week of vigorous-intensity activity (running, HIIT, sports), OR
- An equivalent combination of moderate and vigorous activity.
- Muscle-strengthening activities 2+ days/week (all major muscle groups).
- Limit sedentary time. Replace sitting with any activity, even light.
Adults 65+ years: Same as above, plus balance and fall prevention exercises 3+ days/week.
Children 5-17 years: 60+ min/day of moderate-to-vigorous activity. Vigorous 3+ days/week.
Key finding: Any amount of physical activity is better than none. Even 5-10 min walks provide benefits. Source: WHO Guidelines 2020.

chunks.title: Daily Steps Guidelines and Health Benefits
chunks.content:
Steps per day and mortality risk (meta-analysis, Lancet 2022):
- <4,000 steps/day: Baseline risk (sedentary)
- 4,000-8,000 steps/day: 40-50% reduced all-cause mortality
- 8,000-12,000 steps/day: 50-65% reduced mortality (diminishing returns above 10,000)
- >12,000 steps/day: Marginal additional benefit
The popular '10,000 steps' target originated from a Japanese marketing campaign (1965 pedometer), not scientific research. For most adults, 7,000-8,000 steps/day provides most of the health benefit.
Walking speed matters: Brisk walking (100+ steps/min) provides more cardiovascular benefit than slow walking. Even 10-min daily brisk walks reduce disease risk. Source: Lancet Public Health (2022), Paluch et al., JAMA Internal Medicine.

chunks.title: Health Risks of Prolonged Sitting
chunks.content:
Sedentary behaviour research (Lancet 2016 meta-analysis, >1 million adults):
- Sitting >8 hours/day without physical activity increases all-cause mortality by 59%.
- 60-75 min/day of moderate activity eliminates the increased mortality risk from prolonged sitting.
- Even without formal exercise, breaking up sitting every 30-60 minutes with 2-5 minutes of light activity (standing, walking) improves blood sugar, blood pressure, and triglycerides.
- Prolonged sitting increases risk of: cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, deep vein thrombosis, back pain, obesity, depression.
- Screen time >6 hours/day (beyond work) associated with higher mortality independent of physical activity. Source: Ekelund et al. (2016) Lancet, Patterson et al., AHA Scientific Statement.
